audit_status.source_base: Greek 1 Corinthians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for collection, travel plans, Timothy/Apollos, watchfulness, love, Stephanas household, final greetings and grace.

verses.restored: Now concerning the collection for the holy ones: just as I directed the assemblies of Galatia, so also you do.
On the first day of every week, let each of you set aside by oneself, storing up whatever one may prosper, so that collections may not happen when I come.
When I arrive, whomever you approve through letters, I will send these to carry your grace-gift to Jerusalem.
And if it is suitable for me also to go, they will go with me.
I will come to you when I pass through Macedonia, for I am passing through Macedonia,
and perhaps I will remain with you, or even winter, so that you may send me forward wherever I may go.
For I do not want to see you now in passing, for I hope to remain some time with you, if the Lord permits.
But I will remain in Ephesus until Pentecost,
for a great and effective door has opened to me, and there are many opposing.
If Timothy comes, see that he may be with you without fear, for he works the work of the Lord as I also do.
Therefore let no one despise him, but send him forward in peace, so that he may come to me; for I await him with the siblings.
Now concerning Apollos the sibling, I urged him much to come to you with the siblings, and it was not at all his will to come now; but he will come when he has opportunity.
Stay awake; stand firm in the trust; be courageous; be strong.
Let all your things happen in love.
I urge you, siblings: you know the household of Stephanas, that it is firstfruit of Achaia, and that they appointed themselves into service for the holy ones.
That you also be subject to such ones and to everyone working together and laboring.
I rejoice over the arrival of Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus, because these filled up what was lacking from you,
for they refreshed my spirit and yours. Therefore recognize such ones.
The assemblies of Asia greet you. Aquila and Prisca greet you much in the Lord, with the assembly in their house.
All the siblings greet you. Greet one another with a holy kiss.
The greeting is by my hand, Paul's.
If anyone does not love the Lord, let that one be devoted to judgment. Marana tha.
The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.
My love is with all of you in Messiah Jesus. Amen.

translation_notes: Chapter 16 grounds the letter's spiritual teaching in practical solidarity: collection for Jerusalem, travel, recognition of servants, and love governing all.
Marana tha is preserved as Aramaic liturgical cry, often understood as Our Lord, come / Our Lord has come.
